name: golang-github-spf13-viper-dev
title: golang-github-spf13-viper-dev
description:
Go configuration with fangs
Viper is a complete configuration solution for Go applications
including 12-Factor apps.  It is designed to work within an application,
and can handle all types of configuration needs and formats.
It supports:
.
* setting defaults
* reading from JSON, TOML, YAML, HCL, and Java properties config files
* live watching and re-reading of config files (optional)
* reading from environment variables
* reading from remote config systems (etcd or Consul), and watching changes
* reading from command line flags
* reading from buffer
* setting explicit values
.
Viper can be thought of as a registry for all of your applications
configuration needs.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-spf13-viper-dev because Go configuration with fangs.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-spf13-viper-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-spf13-viper-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-spf13-viper-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
